roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: Slim profiles in matte black, brushed silver, or natural wood. Float frames for canvas. Single mat or no mat.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Monochromatic or analogous palettes. Bold accent colors work as statement pieces.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: Large single pieces or triptychs. Avoid cluttered gallery walls.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Modern rooms pair best with bold single art statements. Frame should not compete with the art.

roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: Ornate or stepped profiles in gold, espresso, or walnut. Double mats with accent reveal.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Rich, warm palettes. Complement existing wood tones. Gold and bronze accents.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: Medium to large pieces with generous mat proportions.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Traditional rooms benefit from museum-style framing with wider mats and ornate mouldings.

roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: Reclaimed wood look, whitewashed, or distressed finishes. Simple profiles. Barn wood frames.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Soft, muted tones. Sage green, warm white, linen, soft blue, natural wood.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: Small to medium pieces, gallery wall groupings work well.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Pair with natural materials for authenticity. Weathered and distressed finishes add character.

roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: Thin profiles in walnut, teak, or brass. Simple clean lines. No ornate detail.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Warm earth tones, mustard, teal, burnt orange. Pop-color accents work well.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: Medium statement pieces at eye level. Horizontal orientation preferred.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Wood tones should echo the room's furniture wood. Brass or gold thin frames complement well.

roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: Ultra-thin frames or floating frames in white, light wood, or black. Clean edges.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Muted, monochromatic. Black and white photography ideal. Soft pastels as accent.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: One large statement piece. Never overcrowd. White space is essential.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Less is more. White space around the art is as important as the art itself.

roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: Raw metal, black steel, reclaimed wood. Wide profiles for impact. Shadow box frames.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Dark tones, metallics, concrete grays. Pops of bold color as accent.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: Oversized pieces or bold gallery walls with mixed frame sizes and styles.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Art should hold its own against exposed brick, metal, and concrete. Bold and graphic works best.

roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: White or light wood frames. Whitewashed or driftwood finishes. Simple profiles.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Blues, sandy tones, seafoam, coral, white. Ocean-inspired palette.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: Medium pieces or relaxed groupings. Horizontal orientation for seascapes.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Art should evoke calm and openness. Light and airy framing to complement coastal vibe.

roomStyleGuides.framingGuidance: Eclectic mix of frame styles. Gold, warm wood, painted frames. Mix widths and finishes.
roomStyleGuides.colorGuidance: Rich warm tones, jewel tones, earth tones. Layered color. Turquoise, amber, burgundy.
roomStyleGuides.sizeGuidance: Gallery walls with varied sizes. Layered and organic arrangement.
roomStyleGuides.notes: Embrace variety. Mix framed and unframed, different media. More is more in bohemian style.
